title: Star Wars: A Pop-Up Guide to the Galaxy
content:
{{Top|rwm|leg|ref|italics=1}} {{ReferenceBook |image=[[File:Pop-Up_Guide.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars:
A Pop-Up Guide to the Galaxy'' |author=[[Matthew Reinhart]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |release date=[[October 15]], [[2007]]{{ThisWeek|DpATvwrcrUw|The Mandalorian Character Posters, Life Day Celebrations with LEGO, and More!|sw_url=video/the-mandalorian-character-posters-life-day-celebrations-with-lego-and-more}} |type=Pop-up |pages= |dimensions= |weight= |isbn=9780439882828 |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} '''''Star Wars: A Pop-Up Guide to the Galaxy''''' is a pop-up book by [[Matthew Reinhart]], releeased by [[Scholastic]] on [[October 15]], [[2007]]. Based on the [[Original trilogy|original ''Star Wars'' trilogy]], the book contains 37 pop-ups within 6 spreads, and a limited-edition cloth-bound release of 500 has been released containing a sound chip and an extra collectible pop-up built into its cover. Matthew Reinhart has been a dedicated fan, reader and collector of Star Wars since he was 7 years old, and wanted to pay tribute during the 30th Anniversary of Star Wars. True as possible to Star Wars [[canon]], Reinhart illustrates the book in an intricate cut-paper collage from handmade papers. The first spread details important locations of the galaxy, including pops of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], while the central pop is a massive [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] stomping over the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]]. The second spread details aliens and creatures, including pops of [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]], [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]], [[Tauntaun/Legends|Tauntauns]], [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]], [[Mynock/Legends|Mynocks]] and the others, while the central pop is a ferocious [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]], holding a [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] guard in one claw and slashing at the reader with the other. The third spread details vehicles, including pops of the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]], [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]], [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] and the others, while the central pop is an 3-D paper sculpture of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. ''Star Wars: A Pop-Up Guide to the Galaxy'' received attention in the national media when US president [[Wikipedia:Barack Obama|Barack Obama]] purchased the book for his press secretary's son.{{OfficialBlog|url=2010/03/25/president-obama-buys-star-wars-pop-up-book|text=President Obama Buys ''Star Wars'' Pop-up Book}}{{WebCite|url=https://thecaucus.blogs.nytimes.com/2010/03/25/obama-stops-to-browse-at-a-bookstore|text=Obama Stops to Browse at a Bookstore|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|''The New York Times'']]|archivedate=20200807204452}} {{Book-stub}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars: A Pop-Up Guide to the Galaxy}} {{App |characters= *[[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] *[[Ackmena/Legends|Ackmena]] *[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] *[[Ponda Baba/Legends|Ponda Baba]] *[[BoShek/Legends|BoShek]] *[[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]] *[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] *[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] *[[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] *[[Salacious B.
